readme.text:
Headless asset upload plugin for Anvilkit Studio.

## What it ships

-

for Studio registration and runtime upload handling.
-

for invoking the configured upload adapter and registering the validated result.
-
for reading the plugin runtime registry after
for storing validated asset metadata by

React components for a host-rendered upload button and asset browser.
- Reference upload adapters for tests and demos.

## Trust model
is treated as untrusted input. Every adapter response is validated through
before it reaches the registry or IR-shaped data, with a default allowlist of

— fixtures for downstream plugin tests.

## Batch upload behaviour
(default 3) uploads in parallel. Per-file failures surface via the listener as
envelopes; the returned promise resolves with the successful subset and **does not throw**. Hosts that need fail-fast semantics can pass

s thrown from the adapter abort the whole batch — pending files are not scheduled.

## S3 presigned adapter
back, then PUTs the file to

. Both phases retry on 5xx and network errors with exponential backoff. 4xx responses fail without retry.

If the host endpoint is **not** S3-compatible (e.g. a custom uploader without overwrite semantics), pass

to disable retry and avoid the small chance of a duplicate upload.

The adapter never logs file contents — only

| The registry has no entry for the asset id.       |
|

| The stored URL failed the allowlist or trust gate. |
